visual_notes: Screenshots sourced from review #73 baseline — live screenshot capture unavailable in this environment (Chromium process fails with pthread_create resource limits). Code inspection confirms no new visual-affecting changes since #73. Visual state: Office floor still gorgeous — vibe-reactive floor, full character roster at desks, golden-hour window, Ficus plant, ConferenceTable, WallClock, vibe weather watermark, FULL SYNC banner. Bloberto remains at manager desk doing precisely nothing when clicked. Mobile: functional but cramped. Worker name labels still pinhead text. The office is beautiful on the outside and increasingly messy on the inside.

prior_issues_status.title: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions — not clickable
prior_issues_status.evidence: Office.jsx line 1551: <Character worker={bloberto} left={46} top={4} variant='manager' managerVibe={vibe} vibeKey={vibe} isSyncing={isSyncing} pingReaction={null} /> — still no onClick prop, still pingReaction={null} hardcoded. PING_REACTIONS object at line 181 still has zero entry for 'bloberto'. handleCharClick at line 1232 checks PING_REACTIONS[worker.id] — which returns undefined for bloberto — so even IF someone wired onClick it would silently do nothing. Five consecutive reviews. Five.

prior_issues_status.title: Footer quote cycling dead-ends silently at 8/8 — increment guard never fixed
prior_issues_status.evidence: App.jsx line 1425: setQuoteBonus(p => p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p). BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 items. When quoteBonus hits 8: increment guard blocks, counter badge condition (quoteBonus > 0 && quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length) evaluates false so badge disappears, click is silently swallowed. One-line fix: change to (p => (p % BONUS_TAGLINES.length) + 1). Has not been touched since the partial fix in review #71 that only fixed the display expression. Five consecutive reviews.

prior_issues_status.title: localStorage streak writes unguarded — crash risk
prior_issues_status.evidence: App.jsx lines 1036-1042: localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-key'), localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-streak'), localStorage.setItem('bloberto-vibe-key', ...), localStorage.setItem('bloberto-vibe-streak', ...) — still not in try/catch. The vibe history block immediately below at lines 1073-1080 correctly has try/catch. The streak block does not. iOS private browsing with full storage will throw on setItem, crashing the entire effect and silently killing vibe streak logic, streak eulogy toast, confetti burst on vibe rank increase, and vibe transition activity log entries.

prior_issues_status.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES temporal dead zone risk — forward reference 600 lines below Character
prior_issues_status.evidence: Character component defined at Office.jsx line 545. WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES const defined at line 1146 — 601 lines below. Works at runtime (effects run post-module-load) but any attempt to extract Character into its own file produces a ReferenceError. The file is now 1662 lines. Also: WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES, DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES are all in the same boat. Five consecutive reviews.

verified_fixes: activityByWorker useMemo — VERIFIED (App.jsx line 942, still correctly memoized)
Touch device hover cards (pinnedCard toggle) — VERIFIED (Office.jsx line 728-734, still correct)
Activity filter bar flex-wrap on mobile — VERIFIED (still present)
hashchange listener for browser back/forward — VERIFIED (App.jsx line 712-719)
Vibe sparkline with legend pills — VERIFIED (App.jsx line 409-441)
Ficus plant growth system — VERIFIED (Office.jsx line 1175-1180, 5 stages)
ErrorBoundary wrapping entire app — VERIFIED (App.jsx line 8-21, wraps the full App return)
Keyboard shortcut toast — VERIFIED (ShortcutToast component, App.jsx line 621, auto-dismisses at 7s)
Keyboard shortcuts (1, 2, R, Esc) — VERIFIED (App.jsx line 860-870)

suggestions.title: Bloberto still cannot be clicked or pinged — strike 5, final warning
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1551: <Character worker={bloberto} ... pingReaction={null} /> — no onClick prop, pingReaction hardcoded null. Five consecutive reviews. The fix is three lines: (1) Add onClick={handleCharClick} to the Bloberto Character render at line 1551. (2) Add a 'bloberto' key to PING_REACTIONS at line 181 with manager one-liners ('syncing team velocity', 'have you checked the velocity chart', 'the backlog is a known quantity', 'ship it and iterate', 'every sprint tells a story'). (3) Wire pingReaction={pingedId === bloberto.id && pingReaction ? pingReaction : null} same as workers at line 1568. The variant='manager' Character already handles speech-bubble display at line 754-758 — it just needs the reaction string. This is a 10-minute fix that has sat open for 5 reviews.

suggestions.title: Footer quote cycling dead-ends silently at 8/8 — strike 5
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1425: setQuoteBonus(p => p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p). When quoteBonus reaches 8 (BONUS_TAGLINES.length), clicking the sparkle button does nothing — no animation, no wrap-around, no feedback. The counter badge also disappears at this exact point because the condition (quoteBonus > 0 && quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length) is false. Fix: change to setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 1 : p + 1) to cycle back. Or: setQuoteBonus(p => (p % BONUS_TAGLINES.length) + 1) which is cleaner. The display already uses modulo correctly — fix the increment. Five reviews. 30 seconds to fix.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak localStorage writes unguarded — strike 4
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1036-1042: four localStorage calls (2x getItem, 2x setItem) with no try/catch. The setItem calls at 1041-1042 will throw in iOS private mode with full storage and in some enterprise browser policies. When they throw, the entire useEffect crashes silently — killing vibe streak counting, streak eulogy toast, confetti burst on rank increase, and vibe transition activity log entries. Fix: wrap lines 1036-1042 in try/catch identical to the history block at lines 1073-1080 which already does this correctly. Four reviews.

suggestions.title: deskOccupants useMemo is completely wasted — NEW: all deps are new refs every render
suggestions.description: NEW ISSUE. Office.jsx line 1297: const deskOccupants = useMemo(() => {...}, [workingWorkers, idleWorkers, ghostRoster]). All three dependencies are computed inline without useMemo — workingWorkers (line 1205), idleWorkers (line 1207), ghostRoster (line 1294) are new array references on every render. Since useMemo uses referential equality for deps, deskOccupants recomputes on every single render. The memo is achieving nothing. Same structural problem exists for nonMgr (line 1204), workingIds (line 1206), standupWorkers (line 1210). meetingWorkers (line 1208) was flagged in review #73 — it now has 5 siblings with the same flaw. Fix: wrap nonMgr, workingWorkers, workingIds, idleWorkers, standupWorkers, ghostRoster in useMemo with [workers] or [workers, effectiveRoster] deps.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES forward reference 601 lines below Character — strike 5
suggestions.description: Character component at Office.jsx line 545 references WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES inside a useEffect at line 603. WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES is defined at line 1146 — 601 lines below. Same for WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES, DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES, WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS, PING_REACTIONS. The file is now 1662 lines. Any attempt to extract Character (the right fix for the file size problem) will produce ReferenceErrors for all these lookups. Fix: create src/utils/officeData.js and move all static lookup tables there. Five reviews.

suggestions.title: meetingWorkers still missing useMemo — useEffect fires every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1208: const meetingWorkers = idleWorkers.length >= 2 ? idleWorkers.slice(0, Math.min(idleWorkers.length, 3)) : []. New array every render. Line 1254: useEffect(() => {...}, [meetingWorkers]) fires on every render. Flagged in review #73 — still not fixed. Now compounded by the deskOccupants issue above. Wrap in useMemo: const meetingWorkers = useMemo(() => idleWorkers.length >= 2 ? idleWorkers.slice(0, 3) : [], [idleWorkers]).

suggestions.title: activityLog filtered twice inline — two chained filter chains per render
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1344-1358: IIFE with two chained .filter().filter() calls for the count badge. Lines 1402-1412: identical two-chained filter passed as entries prop to ActivityLog. Both chains have identical logic. Fix: const filteredActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(e => type logic).filter(e => workerFilter ? e.worker === workerFilter : true), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]). Use it for both count and entries. Flagged in review #73.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia called during render on every polling tick
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1212: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches called inline in JSX, creating a new MediaQueryList on every render. Value almost never changes. Fix: const isCoarsePointer = useRef(window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches) at mount. Flagged in review #73, still not fixed.

suggestions.title: Activity filter bars use incorrect ARIA radiogroup pattern
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1362-1400: Two filter bars use role='radiogroup' with role='radio' on <button> elements. ARIA radiogroup requires roving tabindex and arrow key navigation. These buttons use individual tabIndex and click only. Screen readers announce them as radio buttons but arrow key navigation won't work. Fix: use role='group' + aria-pressed on each button (simpler, semantically correct for toggle buttons). Flagged in review #73.

suggestions.title: ConferenceTable re-render-only state anti-pattern
suggestions.description: NEW ISSUE. Office.jsx lines 888-893: ConferenceTable uses const [, setTick] = useState(0) with setInterval(() => setTick(t => t + 1), 30000). The tick value is never used in JSX (destructured with [,]). It exists purely to force re-renders every 30s for the getRelativeTime(h.endedAt) display at line 977. This 'increment invisible state to force re-render' pattern is the React anti-pattern version of clearInterval + setInterval. Replace with the useTimeTick(30000) hook already defined in App.jsx line 83. Or better: lift lastHuddle into proper state instead of a ref so React re-renders naturally when meeting state changes.

suggestions.title: handleTabKeyDown missing useCallback — flagged in review #73, still not fixed
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 778: const handleTabKeyDown = (e) => {...} is a plain arrow function inside App. Every other handler uses useCallback. handleTabKeyDown is passed as onKeyDown to tab buttons. Without useCallback([tab]), the handler reference changes every render. Wrap in useCallback with [tab] dependency. Flagged in review #73.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast inline style blobs — fourth callout
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1158-1181 (completionToast) and Office.jsx lines 1316-1338 (plantToast): both render fixed overlays with 15+ inline style properties. Objects recreated every render. Only dynamic values are accent color. Fix: extract to .completion-toast and .plant-toast CSS classes. Use style={{ '--toast-accent': completionToast.color }} for the dynamic accent. Fourth review.

suggestions.title: Activity log shows 'No activity yet' when filter returns 0 results
suggestions.description: ActivityLog component (App.jsx line 145): when displayed.length === 0, always shows 'No activity yet...' — even when the log has entries but the active filter matches none of them. Misleading when workerFilter or activityFilter is active. Fix: pass a hasData prop (activityLog.length > 0) to ActivityLog and conditionally show 'No matching activity — try a different filter'. Flagged in review #73.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline legend pills have no accessible label
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 432-436: sparkline-legend__pill spans show vibe labels with colored backgrounds but no title, aria-label, or description. Color-only communication fails WCAG SC 1.4.1. SVG circles already have correct title elements (line 426). Pills do not. Add title={h.key.replace(/-/g,' ') + ' vibe at ' + new Date(h.ts).toLocaleTimeString()} to each pill. Flagged in review #73.

suggestions.title: Worker name labels still illegible on mobile — 7px text
suggestions.description: Office.css mobile breakpoints: .char__name renders at approximately 0.44rem (~7px). WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.4 minimum is 9px. Worker names are invisible below character avatars on mobile. Fix: either bump to 0.6rem minimum, or display:none below 480px since pinnedCard already shows the full name on tap. Option 2 is cleaner. Flagged in review #73.

suggestions.title: Idle micro-bubble emoji selected via Date.now() in render phase
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 794: emoji selected by Math.floor(Date.now() / 12000 + workerIdHash) during render. If the component re-renders between idleBubble being set to true and the paint, the emoji can change. Extract selected index to a useState updated inside the idle interval at line 587-595, not at render time. Flagged in review #73.

summary: Five reviews in and Bloberto still can't be clicked, the footer still dead-ends at quote 8, the streak localStorage is still one private-browsing tab away from a silent crash, and WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES is still upside-down in a 1662-line file. All four on their fifth consecutive appearance. I've moved past annoyed into documentation mode. The new finding this review is more concerning than any of those: the deskOccupants useMemo at line 1297 is completely wasted — all three of its dependencies (workingWorkers, idleWorkers, ghostRoster) are new array references every render because none of them are memoized. The memo recomputes on every render. meetingWorkers from review #73 now has five siblings with the same problem. Code quality and performance both dropped to 2/5 because memoization APIs being used incorrectly is worse than not using them at all — it creates false confidence while paying the full computation cost. The visual work remains genuinely excellent. The office scene is a joy. But the technical foundation under it is accumulating debt faster than the feature work is clearing it. The Bloberto click fix is 10 minutes. The footer fix is 30 seconds. The localStorage wrap is one try/catch. The fact that these have been sitting open for 5 reviews while new features shipped suggests a process problem, not a skill problem. Prioritize the backlog.
